title: pgxgen configuration
description: Configuration file for pgxgen — code generator for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite. Replaces both pgxgen.yaml and sqlc.yaml.

version.description: Config schema version. Must be "2". Use `pgxgen migrate` to upgrade legacy v1 configs.

schemas.description: List of schema sources. Each schema is an independent generation unit with its own engine, migrations, models, and tables. Multiple schemas allow mixing engines (e.g. PostgreSQL + SQLite) in one project.

templates.description: Optional user-provided template directories that override the built-in templates for CRUD SQL and Go models.

crud_dir.description: Directory containing custom CRUD SQL templates. Files in this directory replace the built-in templates of the same name.

models_dir.description: Directory containing custom Go model templates. Files in this directory replace the built-in templates of the same name.

name.description: Human-readable schema name. Used in logs and to disambiguate output when multiple schemas are configured.

engine.description: Database engine. Controls SQL dialect, type mapping, and the sqlc engine selected when generating sqlc.yaml.

schema_dir.description: Path to the directory (or single file) with SQL migration / schema files. Files are parsed in lexicographic order to derive table and column metadata.

models.description: Settings for generating Go model structs from the parsed schema.

sqlc.description: Settings for the auto-generated sqlc.yaml and the sqlc invocation.

defaults.description: Defaults inherited by every table in this schema (paths, CRUD methods, constants).

tables.description: Per-table configuration keyed by table name. Each entry merges with `defaults` and may override CRUD methods, constants, sqlc, and soft-delete settings.

custom_queries.description: Free-form SQL queries that are emitted alongside generated CRUD. Useful for joins, reports, or any query that does not fit the CRUD model.

output_dir.description: Directory where the generated Go models file will be written.

output_file_name.description: Name of the generated models file. Defaults to `models.go`.

package_name.description: Go package name declared at the top of the generated models file.

package_path.description: Full Go import path of the models package (e.g. `github.com/your-org/project/internal/models`). Used by sqlc overrides and by other generated code that needs to import these models.

custom_types.description: Names of types defined manually in the models package. The generator skips emitting these names so your hand-written definitions are preserved.

skip_tables.description: Table or view names to exclude from model generation (e.g. internal migration tables).

skip_enums.description: Enum type names to exclude from model generation.

emit_json_tags.description: Add `json:"…"` struct tags to generated models.

emit_db_tags.description: Add `db:"…"` struct tags to generated models (useful for sqlx, scany, etc).

emit_pointers_for_null.description: Use Go pointer types (`*T`) for nullable columns instead of nullable wrappers like `pgtype.Text` or `sql.NullString`.

include_struct_comments.description: Add `// @name StructName` comments above generated structs (useful for swaggo / Swagger generators).

sql_package.description: SQL package used to choose the nullable / array type families in generated models (e.g. `pgtype.Text` vs `sql.NullString`).

type_overrides.description: Override the SQL → Go type mapping for the models generator. Affects only model structs (sqlc has its own `sqlc.overrides.types`).

go_type.description: Fully-qualified Go type to use (e.g. `github.com/google/uuid.UUID` or just `string`).

import.description: Optional explicit import path. If omitted, it is inferred from `go_type` when it contains a package path.

custom_tags.description: Additional struct tags emitted on every generated model field. Combined with `emit_json_tags` / `emit_db_tags`.

format.description: Tag value format. Applied only to NOT NULL columns (e.g. `required` to emit `validate:"required"`).

keep_generated_config.description: Keep `.pgxgen/sqlc.yaml` after the run. When false (default), the generated config and its containing directory are removed on both success and failure.

sql_package.description: sqlc `sql_package` option — selects the driver-specific nullable / array types in generated sqlc code.

emit_prepared_queries.description: sqlc: emit prepared queries for performance.

emit_interface.description: sqlc: emit a `Querier` interface alongside the concrete `Queries` struct.

emit_json_tags.description: sqlc: add `json:"…"` struct tags to generated rows / params.

emit_db_tags.description: sqlc: add `db:"…"` struct tags to generated rows / params.

emit_exported_queries.description: sqlc: export the constants holding the SQL text of each query.

emit_exact_table_names.description: sqlc: keep table names exact (no singularization). Off by default.

emit_empty_slices.description: sqlc: emit empty slices instead of `nil` for `:many` queries that match no rows.

emit_result_struct_pointers.description: sqlc: return `*Row` instead of `Row` from generated queries.

emit_params_struct_pointers.description: sqlc: accept `*Params` instead of `Params` for generated queries with multi-field params.

emit_enum_valid_method.description: sqlc: add a `Valid()` method on generated enum types.

emit_all_enum_values.description: sqlc: emit a slice with all values for each generated enum.

query_parameter_limit.description: sqlc: maximum number of positional query parameters before sqlc switches the function signature to a single struct param.

json_tags_case_style.description: sqlc: case style for `json:` tag values (`camel`, `pascal`, `snake`, or `none`).

defaults.description: Default sqlc `gen.go` options applied to every generated repo. Maps 1:1 to sqlc's options of the same name.

rename.description: Rename generated identifiers (e.g. `{ d: Params }` renames a struct named `D` to `Params`).

types.description: Override the Go type generated for a SQL type, optionally constrained to nullable columns.

go_type.description: Go type to use. Either a fully-qualified string (`github.com/google/uuid.UUID`) or an object with explicit `type` and `import` keys.

nullable.description: If true, the override only applies to nullable columns of `db_type`.

columns.description: Per-column overrides (e.g. add struct tags or change Go type for `users.email`).

go_type.description: Override the Go type for this specific column. String or object form, same shape as `types[].go_type`.

go_struct_tag.description: Extra struct tag appended to this column's field (e.g. `validate:"required,email"`).

overrides.description: Maps 1:1 to the sqlc `overrides` section. Lets you rename generated types and remap SQL types or specific columns.

sqlc.description: Settings consumed when pgxgen generates `.pgxgen/sqlc.yaml` and runs sqlc.

queries_dir_prefix.description: Per-table layout: queries directory is computed as `{prefix}/{table_name}`. Mutually exclusive with `queries_dir`. Not affected by `package_prefix` / `package_suffix`.

output_dir_prefix.description: Per-table layout: sqlc output directory is computed as `{prefix}/{package_prefix}{table_name}{package_suffix}`. Mutually exclusive with `output_dir`.

package_prefix.description: Per-table layout: prefix prepended to the table name when forming the output directory and Go package name (e.g. `repo_`). Does not affect `queries_dir_prefix`.

package_suffix.description: Per-table layout: suffix appended to the table name when forming the output directory and Go package name (e.g. `_repo`). Does not affect `queries_dir_prefix`.

queries_dir.description: Single-repo layout: directory where every table's queries are placed. Mutually exclusive with `queries_dir_prefix`.

output_dir.description: Single-repo layout: directory where every table's sqlc output is written. Mutually exclusive with `output_dir_prefix`.

auto_clean.description: Remove stale generated CRUD files that no longer correspond to a configured method.

exclude_table_name.description: Drop the table name from generated method names (e.g. `GetByID` instead of `GetUserByID`).

methods.description: Default method definitions applied to every table. Per-table `tables.{name}.crud.methods` overrides these key-by-key.

crud.description: Default CRUD generation settings inherited by every table.

constants.description: Default constants generation settings inherited by every table.

defaults.description: Defaults inherited by every table in the schema. Choose one of two layouts: per-table repos (use `*_prefix`) OR a single shared repo (use `queries_dir` / `output_dir`).

primary_column.description: Name of the primary-key column. Used by CRUD methods like `get`, `update`, `delete` to build the WHERE clause when no explicit `where` is set.

queries_dir.description: Override the queries directory for this table (otherwise resolved from `defaults.queries_dir` / `defaults.queries_dir_prefix`).

output_dir.description: Override the sqlc output directory for this table (otherwise resolved from `defaults.output_dir` / `defaults.output_dir_prefix`).

sqlc.description: Per-table sqlc options that override `sqlc.defaults` for this table only.

methods.description: CRUD methods to generate for this table. Method keys are well-known: `create`, `update`, `delete`, `get`, `find`, `total`, `exists`, `batch_create`. An empty object (`{}`) enables a method with default settings.

column.description: Name of the soft-delete timestamp column (commonly `deleted_at`).

soft_delete.description: Enable soft-delete behavior. The `delete` method becomes an UPDATE that sets the column to `now()`; `get` and `find` automatically add `WHERE {column} IS NULL`.

table.description: Per-table overrides. Anything omitted here is inherited from the schema's `defaults`.

name.description: Override the generated Go method name (e.g. `GetByID`).

returning.description: RETURNING clause for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE (PostgreSQL only). Use `*` to return all columns.

where.description: WHERE conditions keyed by column name. An empty object (`{}`) means `column = $N`. Use `value`/`operator` to customize.

value.description: Right-hand side of the condition. Defaults to the next positional parameter (`$N`). Set to a literal (e.g. `'active'`) or expression to override.

operator.description: Comparison operator. Defaults to `=`. Common values: `=`, `!=`, `>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`, `IN`, `LIKE`, `IS NOT`.

where_additional.description: Additional raw SQL conditions appended to the WHERE clause (e.g. `"status = 'active'"`). Combined with AND.

skip_columns.description: Columns to omit from this method's INSERT/UPDATE column list (e.g. `[id, created_at]`).

column_values.description: Override the generated value for specific columns with a literal SQL expression (e.g. `{ created_at: "now()" }`). Such columns are filled by SQL instead of Go.

limit.description: For `find`: emit `LIMIT $N OFFSET $N` and accept the corresponding parameters.

method.description: CRUD method definition. `null` or `{}` enables the method with default settings derived from the table.

type.description: sqlc query annotation: `one` (single row), `many` (slice of rows), `exec` (no result), `copyfrom` (PostgreSQL COPY FROM).

table.description: Optional table name. When set, the query is emitted into that table's resolved `queries_dir` and uses its row type.

output_dir.description: Explicit output directory for the query. Takes precedence over `table`.

sql.description: The SQL body. Use `$1`, `$2`, … for PostgreSQL parameters or `?` for MySQL/SQLite.

custom_query.description: Free-form SQL query emitted alongside generated CRUD.
